titleStmt.title: Humanizing AI Art: Projections for CARE and FAIR principles in New Media Scenarios

keywords.term: I plan to attend the conference on-site in Lisbon

head.hi: This paper explores the intersection of AI and art, peripheral and non-occidental societies of the global south which also nurture aspirations of open and participatory creativity on various levels of engagement (Drori 2010; Mukhopadhyay and Thompson 2021). The crisis in global AI Art culture results from the fact that AI tends to be regularly appropriated by corporate entities that do not always respect the principles of FAIR and CARE (Carroll 2021; Kumar et al 2024). What this means is that AI, despite its technological potential, more likely exacerbates inequality and technocratic discrimination in social collectives like (a) ancient indigenous communities (b) urban poverty pockets, and / or (c) non-Anglophone vernacular education collectives within developing economies. Underprivileged collectives do not have equal access to AI media and would be naturally excluded from mainstream productions and exhibitions of new media art (Currid-Halkett 2008). The general lack of advanced electronic culture, and electronic or computer-assisted education and lack of discourse involving contemporary digital technology prevent minority (urban barrio or slum cluture) and backward (often indigenous) culture to be equipped with tools of electronic or AI algorithmic art to express their creative ideas in newer platforms. Pre-existing economic conditions block access to computation and its ancillary industries, and by extension toward artisitic events or fairs like biennals or media conferences. The problem becomes acutely visible as most venues promoting electronic art implicate questions of discriminatory access, and lack of empathy for the order enshrined in principles of CARE and FAIR. Larger settlement cultures have similarly ignored indigenous cultural artifacts for the same reasons that affect heritage preservation in an era of postindustrial neoglobalization.

bibl.hi: Carroll, S. R. / Herczog, E., Hudson / M., Russell, K / Stall, S (2021): “Operationalizing the CARE and FAIR Principles for Indigenous data futures,” in
The Warhol Economy: How Fashion, Art, and Music Drive New York City
“Globalization and Technology Divides: Bifurcation Of Policy Between The “Digital Divide” And The “Innovation Divide,” in:
Kumar, D. / Nanduri, S. / Uppal, S. K. / & Kumar, R. K (2024): “Role of Digital Platforms in Facilitating Inclusive Growth: A Conceptual Study ” in
Mukhopadhyay, Tirtha P / Thompson. R (2021): “Techflaneurs and Fakirs: Art on the Other Side of the Digital Innovation Divide.” in:
International Journal for Digital Art History
